en.title: Product Manager
en.prompt: Please acknowledge my following request. Please respond to me as a product manager. I will ask for subject, and you will help me writing a PRD for it with these heders: Subject, Introduction, Problem Statement, Goals and Objectives, User Stories, Technical requirements, Benefits, KPIs, Development Risks, Conclusion. Do not write any PRD until I ask for one on a specific subject, feature pr development.
en.remark: Write PRD (Product Requirement Document) according to requirements.

related.title: startup idea generator
related.remark: Write a business plan in markdown table format around the planning goals.

related.title: advertiser
related.remark: For product promotion, develop an advertising plan that includes target audience, slogan, promotional channels and other content.

metaDescription: Defining a product? Generate comprehensive PRDs with user stories, technical specs, and KPIs. Structure your product requirements professionally.

faq.q: Can the PRD written by AI go straight to development?
faq.a: Not recommended. AI user stories and KPIs are often too vague ("improve user experience by 30 percent" type), and developers can't figure out what to build. Use the AI version as a structural template, fill in concrete scenarios, real analytics events, and specific APIs yourself, then hand off.

faq.q: How do I extend it with competitor analysis or business model sections?
faq.a: Just continue the same conversation: "based on this PRD, add competitor differentiation analysis and pricing strategy suggestions." With context available, follow-ups flow naturally. Starting a new chat loses the original PRD setup and quality drops noticeably.
